Report: Inconsistent Behavior of cupy.linspace with numpy.float32 Input","user":{"login":"mjuergensen","id":62663238,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYyNjYzMjM4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/62663238?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mjuergensen","html_url":"https://github.com/mjuergensen","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mjuergensen/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mjuergensen/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mjuergensen/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mjuergensen/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mjuergensen/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mjuergensen/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mjuergensen/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mjuergensen/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mjuergensen/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":472944224,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw0NzI5NDQyMjQ=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/labels/cat:bug","name":"cat:bug","color":"ee0701","default":false,"description":"Bugs"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-05T15:37:26Z","updated_at":"2024-11-05T15:37:26Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description

The linspace function in CuPy exhibits different behavior compared to the linspace function in NumPy when a numpy.float32 datatype is passed. This discrepancy becomes evident in high-precision applications such as high-frequency FFT (Fast Fourier Transform).

Observed Behavior:
When using numpy.float32 as input, the 8th position of the output differs between NumPy and CuPy:

NumPy: t_chirp_np[5] = 1.2506252810628754e-07
CuPy: t_chirp_cp[5] = 1.2506252744515223e-07

This difference, although minor, can lead to significant issues in high-precision computations.

### To Reproduce

```py
import numpy as np
import cupy as cp
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

"""

    implement a sinewave based on cupy linspace and numpy linspace and perform window and fft.
    plot fft.

"""
chirp_period = np.array(50e-6, dtype=np.float32)
f_if_max = 1e9 / chirp_period * 2 * 150 / 3e8
adc_sample_time = 1 / f_if_max / 2


t_chirp_np = np.linspace(0, chirp_period, int(chirp_period / adc_sample_time),
                                   dtype=np.float64)

print(t_chirp_np[5])

t_chirp_cp = cp.linspace(0, chirp_period, int(chirp_period / adc_sample_time),
                                   dtype=cp.float64)

print(t_chirp_cp[5])

t_chirp_cp = cp.asnumpy(t_chirp_cp)

frequency = 0.0001e9

sine_np = np.sin(2 * np.pi * frequency * t_chirp_np)

sine_cp = np.sin(2 * np.pi * frequency * t_chirp_cp)

window = np.hanning(len(sine_np))

sine_np = sine_np * window

sine_cp = sine_cp * window

frequency_spectrum_np = np.fft.fft(sine_np, axis=0) / len(sine_np)
frequencies_np = np.fft.fftfreq(len(sine_np), d=adc_sample_time)
valid = frequencies_np > 0
frequencies_np = frequencies_np[valid]
frequency_spectrum_np = frequency_spectrum_np[valid]

frequency_spectrum_cp = np.fft.fft(sine_cp, axis=0) / len(sine_cp)
frequencies_cp = np.fft.fftfreq(len(sine_cp), d=adc_sample_time)
valid = frequencies_cp > 0
frequencies_cp = frequencies_cp[valid]
frequency_spectrum_cp = frequency_spectrum_cp[valid]

fig = plt.figure()
fig.subplots_adjust(hspace=1)
ax_spec = fig.add_subplot(2, 2, 1)

ax_spec.plot(t_chirp_np, sine_np, ".", markersize=1)

ax_spec.set_xlabel("frequencies")
ax_spec.set_ylabel("amplitude in dB")
ax_spec.set_title("time signal numpy")
ax_spec.grid()

ax_spec = fig.add_subplot(2, 2, 2)

ax_spec.plot(t_chirp_cp, sine_cp, ".", markersize=1)

ax_spec.set_xlabel("frequencies")
ax_spec.set_ylabel("amplitude in dB")
ax_spec.set_title("time signal cupy")
ax_spec.grid()

ax_spec = fig.add_subplot(2, 2, 3)

ax_spec.plot(frequencies_np, 10 * np.log10(abs(frequency_spectrum_np)), ".", markersize=1)

ax_spec.set_xlabel("frequencies")
ax_spec.set_ylabel("amplitude in dB")
ax_spec.set_title("frequency spectrum numpy")
ax_spec.grid()

ax_spec = fig.add_subplot(2, 2, 4)

ax_spec.plot(frequencies_cp, 10 * np.log10(abs(frequency_spectrum_cp)), ".", markersize=1)

ax_spec.set_xlabel("frequencies")
ax_spec.set_ylabel("amplitude in dB")
ax_spec.set_title("frequency spectrum cupy")
ax_spec.grid()

plt.savefig("difference_linspace_numpy_cupy")

print("done")
```

Source (`pip install cupy`)
